diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers gave the same unsupported label and cited no evidence because none of the evidence items mention or directly bear on suppress_new_idea. The difference is in reason-code specificity rather than evidentiary basis. Option 1's central_proposition_unsupported is more directly tied to the claim-level defect: the anchor proposition itself is not supported by any evidence. Option 2's insufficient_evidence is also plausible but broader and less specific. The rationales are substantively equivalent, and both high-confidence judgments fit the clear absence of relevant evidence.
